summary: Overall UK risk remains Elevated. The dominant system-shaping factor remains external security and commodity-price shock risk, with the Bank of England explicitly linking Middle East conflict-driven rises in global energy and other commodity prices to higher near-term CPI inflation and weaker activity. Domestically, disruption risk remains concentrated in public-service delivery (NHS operational pressure during ongoing resident-doctor industrial action) and in cyber exposure for widely deployed edge infrastructure products requiring urgent remediation.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: The Bank of England continues to warn that conflict-driven energy and commodity price rises will lift near-term inflation and weigh on activity; this keeps UK cost-of-living and business-cost sensitivity elevated.
drivers: BoE (March 2026) held Bank Rate at 3.75% and stated Middle East conflict-driven rises in global energy and other commodity prices will raise near-term CPI inflation and can weaken activity: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026

ONS inflation dashboard continues to show CPI at 3.0% y/y in February 2026 (above target), indicating limited room for complacency on household cost pressures: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices
Brent pricing remains a key pass-through channel for UK fuel and utility bills

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: The UK terrorism baseline remains SUBSTANTIAL, and ongoing major conflicts (Russia–Ukraine and the Middle East) sustain elevated spillover risks relevant to UK resilience (energy and wider security uncertainty).
drivers: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely): https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

BoE highlights the Middle East conflict as a driver of higher global energy and commodity prices, reflecting continued geopolitical spillover channels: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026
Ongoing Russia–Ukraine and Middle East conflict environment sustains elevated background security risk

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No new signal in reviewed sources indicates an acute UK governance crisis; current pressure appears focused on managing economic and public-service delivery impacts rather than institutional instability.
drivers: No reviewed official signals of immediate UK constitutional or governance instability
Policy attention remains focused on economic resilience and public-service delivery pressures
External security environment continues to create policy-management load

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: No evidence in reviewed sources today of an imminent UK-wide severe weather emergency; hazard conditions should be monitored via official warning services for time-critical changes.
drivers: No reviewed official evidence today of UK-wide severe hazard escalation
Flood and severe weather conditions can change quickly; rely on official warning services for time-critical updates
Spring transition can still produce local flooding and travel disruption

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Disruption risk remains elevated due to ongoing resident-doctor strike action in England, with expected knock-on impacts for elective activity and operational resilience even where urgent and emergency care is prioritised.
drivers: NHS England: industrial action is scheduled from 07:00 on Tuesday 7 April to 06:59 on Monday 13 April 2026 (England): https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/industrial-action-bma-resident-doctors-april-2026-2/
Strike action increases cancellations/rescheduling and adds to existing service pressure
Operational risk is highest where staffing gaps align with already-tight capacity

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: The advisory environment remains elevated, with urgent patching requirements for widely deployed Citrix NetScaler products and continued exploitation pressure against internet-facing edge systems.
drivers: NCSC urges UK organisations to take immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3055, CVE-2026-4368): https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/ncsc-urges-immediate-action-to-mitigate-citrix-netscaler-vulnerabilities
NetScaler edge appliances are common in enterprise access pathways (VPN/AAA), increasing potential blast radius if unpatched
Perimeter/edge exploitation remains a persistent driver of UK cyber risk

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent at 95.20 USD/bbl (April 10, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (y/y)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (inflation dashboard). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices

indicators.name: Bank Rate (UK)
indicators.note: Bank of England: MPC maintained Bank Rate at 3.75% (March 2026) and noted conflict-driven energy/commodity price rises will increase near-term CPI inflation. https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy-summary-and-minutes/2026/march-2026

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: Resident doctor industrial action (England)
indicators.note: NHS England: industrial action from 7am Tue 7 April to 6:59am Mon 13 April 2026. https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/industrial-action-bma-resident-doctors-april-2026-2/

changes_since_last_update: Brent crude indicator updated using Trading Economics (95.20 USD/bbl, April 10, 2026 observation).
Minor wording updates to reflect the same core drivers; category statuses unchanged.
priority_watch_items: Energy spillovers: monitor for renewed upward pressure on wholesale energy and fuel prices and associated UK inflation re-acceleration.
NHS resilience: monitor strike impacts, elective cancellations, and any escalation to critical incidents/OPEL pressures during the remainder of the action window.
Edge-vulnerability exposure: confirm patch/mitigation for Citrix NetScaler in UK organisations and watch for exploitation signals.
Geopolitical spillovers: monitor for any broadening of Middle East and Russia–Ukraine risks that could affect shipping routes, insurance, or supply.
Severe weather: monitor official flood and weather warnings for fast-changing local disruptions.
source_notes: This update prioritises official UK sources (BoE, ONS, MI5, NHS England, NCSC) plus Trading Economics for a Brent price snapshot.
Some indicators are not real-time; market prices may move intraday and official statistics are published on set schedules.
Where statuses reflect advisory context (e.g., cyber vulnerabilities), they indicate elevated baseline risk rather than confirmed UK national-scale outages.
